exercises.name: Barbell Narrow Grip Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Placing your hands closer together on the barbell, set your knees with a soft bend, hips back and chest up while you perform the bicep curl. 

2) Keep your elbows tucked in while curling.

exercises.name: Barbell Overhead Press
exercises.alternateName: Barbell Standing Shoulder Press military strict
exercises.description:
1) Start by holding a barbell near your upper chest, in a slightly wider than shoulder width overhand grip. 

2) Press the bar overhead until your arms are extended, pause, return to start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Barbell Overhead Push Press
exercises.description:
1) Set the barbell in the front rack position, resting on the front of your shoulders with elbows high. Set your feet slightly wider than hip width. 

2) Give a slight bend in your knees and hips. Extend through your lower body, and follow through with a shoulder press overhead.

3) Your arms should fully extend with the biceps lining up with your ears. Keep your core engaged to prevent extension through the spine, and maintain neutral spine.

4) Bring the barbell back to the front rack position well keeping your glutes and abdominals active.

exercises.name: Barbell Overhead Squat
exercises.description:
1) When performing the overhead squat, ensure that your hips are set roughly shoulder-width apart. 

2) Sit back and ensure that you have a safe back by keeping it neutral or slight arch to it. 

3) The barbell will be overhead, so be sure to keep a straight line lockout with your arms pointing directly to the ceiling at all times. 

4) That means, one part of the lift, you’ll gonna need to push your head and body through to ensure that you keep that line and stay underneath the weight safely.

exercises.name: Barbell Power Snatch
exercises.description:
1) When performing the power snatch, begin by getting into a deadlift position.

2) Make sure your back is set in a neutral or slightly arched position. 

3) From there, drive up through the hips, putting momentum into the barbell. 

4) Follow through with that by shoulder shrugging and swinging the barbell up over top of the head. 

5) Ensure when you lockout in this position, keep your arms fully extended and push your head a little bit through to open up your chest.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, using a wide grip hold an EZ curl bar, palms facing up.

2.Curl the bar upward. Pause.

3.Lower the bar returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
exercises.alternateName: Elbows Out Barbell Row Wide grip
exercises.description:
1.With your knees slightly bent and your back straight, bend over and grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip.

2.Keeping your torso still, pull the barbell up towards your chest until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Pause.

3.Lower the barbell returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Shrug
exercises.description:
1) Stand and hold a barbell behind your back with your palms facing backwards. 

2) Begin by raising your shoulders up as far as possible, pause, and then return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1) Position the Barbell across your upper back holding the ends with your hands to keep it stable. 

2) Stand erect with your chest out and your shoulders back, maintaining the natural curve in your back and knees unlocked. Your feet should be about hip-width apart. This is your starting position.

3) Initiate the movement by stepping back with one foot about 2 feet, keeping your hips facing forward as the toe of your back foot makes contact first. 

4) Then allow both knees to bend and descend just short of allowing your trailing knee to touch the floor.

5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.description:
1) Stand straight holding a barbell in overhand grip with feet hip-width apart. Your arms will need to be kept straight for the full exercise. 

2) Start by lowering the bar down by pushing your hips back as your chest and shoulders move ahead of the bar. 

3) Keep lowering until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings. 

4) Stop when bar is in middle of knees and shin, pause, and get back to starting position.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, set up the rack appropriately.

2. Grab the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing forward).

3. Once you pick up the barbell with the correct grip width, lift the bar up over your head by locking your arms. 

4. Hold at about shoulder level and slightly in front of your head. This is your starting position.

5. Lower the bar down to the shoulders slowly as you inhale.

6. Lift the bar back up to the starting position as you exhale.

7. Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Barbell Shrug
exercises.description:
1) Stand and hold a barbell with both hands in front of you at arms length, with your hands slightly wider than shoulder width apart. 

2) Begin by raising your shoulders up as far as possible, pause, and then return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Barbell Side Bend
exercises.description:
1) Stand straight up with a barbell resting on your shoulders and your back straight. 

2) Begin by bending to one side as far as you can and then to the other side as far as you can, using only your waist.

exercises.name: Barbell Single Leg Hip Thrust
exercises.description:
Sit with your upper back supported on a bench and a barbell across your hips. 

Extend one leg straight out while keeping the other foot flat on the ground. 

Drive through the grounded heel to lift your hips, then lower with control. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: barbell situps
exercises.description: barbell situps

exercises.name: Barbell Skullcrusher
exercises.alternateName: Easy Bar Skullcrusher EZ bar, tricep extension
exercises.description:
1) Start by pressing up the barbell with your grip less than 12 inches apart. 

2) Slowly lower the barbell towards your head, pause, and raise the barbell again.

exercises.name: Barbell Step Up
exercises.description:
1. When performing the step up, ensure that your foot, knee and hip are in line while you step up. At the top of the step, ensure that your hips come underneath you.

2. Stand up straight while holding a barbell placed on the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) and stand upright behind an elevated platform

3. Place the right foot on the elevated platform. And let the left foot stay on the ground. This will be your starting position.

4. Lift your left foot all the way forward while keeping your knee slightly bent. And your right foot straight.

5. Then back to starting position.

6. Once you’re completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Barbell Straight Leg Deadlift
exercises.description:
1) Start by standing with your feet shoulder width apart. 

2) Pick up the barbell by bending knees to bend over and grasp barbell with a shoulder width overhand grip and back straight. 

3) Lift weight to standing position keeping your back straight. 

4) Begin by bending your hips to lower the bar to the top of your feet. 

5) As you descend, make sure to bend your knees slightly with your waist kept straight. 

6) With your knees still bent, lift the bar by extending at your hips until standing upright. Repeat.

exercises.name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
exercises.description:
1.Set your feet much wider than shoulder width. You’ll want to turn them out at about 45 degrees. 

2.During the deadlift, always keep your back set.

3.Take a breath, and then lower your torso pushing your hips back, looking forward with your head with your chest up. 

4.Drive through the floor, spreading your feet apart, with your weight on the back half of your feet. Extend through the hips and knees.

5. As the bar passes through the knees, lean back and drive the hips into the bar.

6.Return the weight to starting position and control the weight on the way down.

exercises.name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
exercises.description:
1.Set your feet much wider than shoulder width. You’ll want to turn them out at about 45 degrees. 

2.During the deadlift, always keep your back set.

3.Take a breath, and then lower your torso pushing your hips back, looking forward with your head with your chest up. 

4.Drive through the floor, spreading your feet apart, with your weight on the back half of your feet. Extend through the hips and knees.

5. As the bar passes through the knees, lean back and drive the hips into the bar.

6.Return the weight to starting position and control the weight on the way down.

exercises.name: Barbell Thruster
exercises.alternateName: BB squat to shoulder overhead press
exercises.description:
1) When performing the thruster, you’ll need to be able to clean the barbell up to your chest and shoulders. 

2) From there, you’ll perform a squat. Make sure that you sit back even though you’re loaded up front with a barbell. 

3) Keep your arms up as high as you can or parallel to the ground. 

4) Pop out of your hips and follow through with your arms. 

5) Repeat.

exercises.name: Barbell Torso Twist
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a PVC pipe or wooden dowel in a wide position grip, placed it onto the back of your neck.

2.Twist your upper torso to one side, keeping your head straight and forward.

3.Twist back to the opposite side, allowing your hips to move.

4.Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Barbell Upright Row
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding the bar with a closed grip, with your arms straight and palms facing back.

2.Raise the bar up to just below your chin, keeping your elbows above your hands, bar close to your body.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Grip Bicep Curl
exercises.alternateName: EZ bar wide grip bicep curl
exercises.description:
1) Set your knees with a soft bend, hips back and chest up while you perform your wide grip bicep curl. 

2) Keep your elbows tucked in while curling.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Grip Deadlift
exercises.description:
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell wider than shoulder width. Lower your hips and keep your back straight. 

Push through your heels to lift the barbell, fully extending your hips and knees at the top. Lower the bar back to the floor in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Grip Romanian Deadlift
exercises.description:
With feet shoulder-width apart, hold the barbell with a wide grip. 

Hinge at the hips to lower the barbell along your legs, keeping your back straight and a slight bend in your knees. 

Stop when you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then return to standing.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Power Slam
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, with a single arm, you will wind up lift the rope high and slam down as hard as you can using your whole body.

2) Then you’ll alternate to the other side, back and forth.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Shoulder Press to Lateral Drop
exercises.alternateName: alternating single arm press to side lower
exercises.description:
1) Set your feet below your hips with a light knee bend. Set your abdominals by drawing them in. Hands holding the battle ropes in the stagecoach grip. 

2) Push one arm overhead in a should press.

3) Alternate between left and right sides for an even number of presses for each side until presceibed repetitions are complete.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’re gonna be sending opposing waves down the rope.

2) One hand will be coming up as the other down. 

3) Then switching back and forth really fast.

4) You’ll want to set yourself in a squat so that you’ll have a solid base, and then really move your arms as fast as you can.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Get Up
exercises.alternateName: battle rope up-down kneel to squat
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll alternate the ropes left to right in opposing directions.

2) While sending those waves down rope, you’ll going to kneel onto your knees and then get back up.

3) Keep in mind that you’ll want to keep your hips on and glutes on so you make a nice tall line throughout your body while you’re in the kneeling position.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll alternate the ropes left to right, one coming up, and then the other down. Reversing back and forth.

2) At the same time, you’ll be stepping back into a reverse lunge.

3) When doing a reverse lunge, and alternating your feet through them, ensure that you’re trying to hit 90 degrees throughout all your lower body segments.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Side Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll alternate the rope left and right in opposing directions.

2) At the same time, you’ll also perform a side lunge. When doing a side lunge, you’ll want to ensure that your foot, knee and hip are all in alignment.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Side Shuffle
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll alternate the rope coming up and down between the left and the right side.

2) While simultaneously side hoping left to right.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Alternating Wave Squat
exercises.description:
1) While performing this exercise, you’ll do an alternating wave, left and right hand moving in opposite directions.

2) While also doing repeating squats simultaneously as the same time as the waves.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Burpee
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll lift the rope as high as you can before slamming down on to the ground.

2) When you do so, try to slam the ropes down using your abdominal as much as you can.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Cross Body Wave
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, set your foundation by getting into a half squat.

2) When you perform the movement with your arm itself, you’ll be lifting the rope straight up and then slamming it down.

3) Try and not to twist your body while performing the exercise.

4) When you’re completed one side, do the same number of reps OR for the same duration of time with the other arm.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Double Wave
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, you’ll want to send waves down on the rope with both arms sending a wave at the same time.

2) If you want to isolate your upper body, you can just use your arms 

3) OR you can make it a full body exercise and include your lower body by pumping through small squats.

exercises.name: Battle Rope High Plank Alternating Slams
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll be on top of the rope with your hand hold grip.

2) Maintain a quiet body that means abdominal engaged and hips not rocking back and forth or lifting up too high.

exercises.name: Battle Rope In And Out Wave
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll alternate opening your arms as wide as you can.

2) Crossing them slightly in front of you. Then, firing the back out.

3) Each time you come to the center, try to alternate which hand is over top of the other.
